channel.title: Hoops Intel — Daily NBA Scores & Analysis
channel.description: Your daily NBA intelligence briefing with scores, player rankings, injury reports, and game previews.

item.title: Detroit Avoids Historic Collapse While Houston Stuns Lakers in Playoff Drama
item.description: Tuesday's playoff action delivered championship-defining moments as Detroit prevented one of the biggest upsets in NBA history by defeating Orlando in Game 4, while Houston shocked Los Angeles on the road to steal home-court advantage in their series opener. — Detroit avoided becoming the first top seed in NBA history to be swept in the first round, defeating Orlando 116-109 at Little Caesars Arena to extend their playoff lives and prevent a historic collapse. The Pistons shot an efficient 47.8% from the field while finally matching the Magic's energy and execution that had dominated the series through three games, delivering the kind of championship-level performance that made them the East's top seed during the regular season.

item.title: Playoff Drama Reaches Fever Pitch as Road Teams Deliver Championship-Defining Statements
item.description: Thursday's playoff action delivered another night of stunning road victories and clutch performances, as visiting teams seized momentum in crucial swing games while stars embraced the pressure of elimination basketball with legendary displays of championship resolve. — Victor Wembanyama exploded for 38 points and 15 rebounds as San Antonio forced Game 7 with a dominant 120-108 road victory at Portland, while Anthony Davis delivered a clutch overtime masterpiece with 35 points to power the Lakers past Houston 112-108 in a series-clinching thriller, and Jayson Tatum's redemptive 31-point performance helped Boston even their series with Philadelphia 1-1 following a 108-100 road victory.

item.title: Playoff Shockwaves Continue as Road Teams Deliver Championship-Defining Performances
item.description: Sixers Complete Stunning Comeback to Eliminate Celtics · Trail Blazers Advance Past First Round After Historic Upset · Lakers Hold Off Rockets Behind Davis' Dominance — Joel Embiid delivered a masterful 32-point, 14-rebound performance that silenced the hostile crowd and eliminated the favored Celtics from championship contention in a decisive Game 7 at TD Garden, completing one of the most remarkable playoff comebacks in recent memory as Philadelphia stunned Boston 111-97

item.title: Thunder Demolish Suns 119-84 in Statement Victory as Shai Scores 29 in Historic Blowout · Celtics Crush 76ers 123-91 Behind Tatum's 35 Points to Strengthen Eastern Conference Standing · Magic Upset Pistons 112-101 on Road as Banchero Dominates with 31 Points
item.description: Heavyweight Saturday — Top Seeds Flex Dominance While Magic Pull Off Stunning Road Upset — Paolo Banchero delivered a career-high 31 points on 60% shooting as Orlando pulled off a stunning 112-101 upset of conference-leading Detroit on the road, while Oklahoma City demolished Phoenix 119-84 and Boston crushed Philadelphia 123-91 in dominant displays by the league's elite teams.

item.title: Judgment Night — Two Eliminations, Two Dreams, and the Final Chapter of the 2026 Play-In Tournament
item.description: Friday's doubleheader decides the last two playoff spots: Charlotte travels to Orlando for the East 8-seed in a LaMelo Ball vs. Paolo Banchero showdown, and Golden State visits Phoenix with Stephen Curry attempting his third consecutive elimination survival. Winners face Detroit and Oklahoma City. Losers go home forever. — Tonight's elimination doubleheader writes the final chapter of the play-in tournament. At Kia Center, Paolo Banchero faces redemption after his 7-22 nightmare while LaMelo Ball rides 30-10 overtime momentum. In the desert, 38-year-old Curry needs another vintage performance against Phoenix's depth. After tonight, the complete 16-team bracket is set and Round 1 tips tomorrow.

item.title: Elimination Excellence — Maxey's 31 Locks East 7-Seed While Curry's Vintage 35 Saves Warriors' Season
item.description: Tyrese Maxey commanded Philadelphia's 109-97 victory over Orlando with 31 points and 6 assists to claim the East 7-seed, while Stephen Curry turned back time with 35 points and seven three-pointers in Golden State's 126-121 survival win that ended the Clippers' season. The 76ers face Boston in Round 1; the Warriors live to fight Friday at Phoenix. — Stephen Curry delivered one of the most poignant performances of his legendary career, scoring 35 points with 7 three-pointers in an elimination game at age 38, just 14 days after returning from a 73-day absence with bilateral knee soreness. His vintage shooting saved Golden State's season and proved greatness has no expiration date.

item.title: Survive and Advance — Play-In Tournament Tips Tonight with Elimination in Charlotte and a Gateway to Wembanyama in Phoenix
item.description: The 82-game regular season is history. Tonight, one team's season ends, another punches its ticket to face the Spurs, and the 2025-26 postseason officially begins. — Play-in tournament tips with two pivotal games on Prime Video. Miami at Charlotte (7:30 ET) is a straight elimination — loser goes home, winner advances to Friday. Portland at Phoenix (10:00 ET) determines who faces Wembanyama's 62-win Spurs in Round 1. Bam Adebayo enters shooting 67% over 5 games; Kon Knueppel brings his record 268 made threes. Flagg closes as -175 ROY favorite after 51-pt and 45-pt final week, while Luka's hamstring clouds the Lakers' title hopes.

item.title: Season Finale: 15 Games to Settle 10 Seeds · Denver W11 vs Wembanyama's Spurs · East 5-8 Goes Down to Wire
item.description: The final day of the 2025-26 regular season features a 15-game Sunday spectacular. Denver's historic 11-game winning streak — the longest of the Jokić era — rides into San Antonio on ESPN with the West's 3-seed at stake. The East's 5-through-8 seeds remain completely unsettled with Atlanta, Toronto, Orlando, and Philadelphia all separated by two games. — No games were played on April 11 as the league took a rest day before the season finale. Tonight's 15-game slate determines one remaining playoff spot and 10 postseason seeds. The marquee matchup sends Denver's 11-game win streak into San Antonio's Frost Bank Center on ESPN at 8:30 PM ET — Jokić vs Wembanyama for the West's 3-seed. Cade Cunningham returned Friday with 26/9 to clinch the East 1-seed for Detroit. Anthony Edwards also returned with 35 points against Houston.

item.title: Thunder Make Championship Statement with 123-87 Lakers Demolition — Pelicans Explode for 156 Points in Historic Shootout — Rockets Hit 50 Wins with Suns Rout — Tonight's Games Decide Final Playoff Picture
item.description: The Oklahoma City Thunder delivered the most dominant performance of the season, obliterating the Los Angeles Lakers 123-87 in a 36-point massacre that showcased their championship credentials. In New Orleans, the Pelicans lit up the scoreboard for 156 points in a wild shootout victory over Utah. Houston hit the 50-win milestone with a statement victory over Phoenix, while tonight's seven-game slate features massive playoff implications with Cleveland-Atlanta and Oklahoma City-LA Clippers leading the way. — Thunder demolish Lakers 123-87 in season's most dominant performance as Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scores 28 points in just 29 minutes

item.title: Wemby 32/10/6/3BLK Conquers OKC — Spurs Seize West 1-Seed With W12 · Luka 36/8/10 Ends Denver's 8-Game Streak · Brunson 28/7 Powers Knicks to 51 Wins · Trae 28/9 Hawks W3 · Barnes 29/8/7 Raptors Bounce Back
item.description: Victor Wembanyama dominates with 32 points, 10 rebounds, 6 assists, and 3 blocks as San Antonio conquers OKC 118-113 to seize the West 1-seed with their 12th straight win; Luka Dončić scores 36 with 8 rebounds and 10 assists as the Lakers end Denver's 8-game winning streak 126-121; Jalen Brunson pours in 28 with 7 assists as the Knicks beat Cleveland 112-105 for their 51st win; Trae Young's 28/9 lifts Atlanta past Miami 114-108 for a third straight win; Scottie Barnes posts 29/8/7 as Toronto bounces back with a 118-104 win over Charlotte — Victor Wembanyama put on a historic two-way performance against the league's best. The 22-year-old posted 32 points, 10 rebounds, 6 assists, and 3 blocks as the Spurs beat OKC 118-113 to seize the Western Conference 1-seed with their 12th consecutive victory. Stephon Castle added 18 points. SGA had 29/6/7 and Chet Holmgren 20/11 in a losing effort. Luka Dončić was sensational with 36 points, 8 rebounds, and 10 assists as the Lakers snapped Denver's 8-game winning streak 126-121. Anthony Davis added 24/12. Nikola Jokić had 30/12/8 and Jamal Murray scored 25 in defeat. Jalen Brunson scored 28 with 7 assists as the Knicks beat Cleveland 112-105 to reach 51 wins. Karl-Anthony Towns added 22/10. Donovan Mitchell had 27 for the Cavs. Trae Young dished 9 assists to go with 28 points as Atlanta beat Miami 114-108 for their third straight win. Jalen Johnson added 22/8. Tyler Herro scored 26 in the loss. Scottie Barnes posted 29/8/7 as Toronto bounced back with a 118-104 win over Charlotte. Kon Knueppel had 21 for the Hornets.

item.title: Wembanyama 28/12/5/4BLK Powers Spurs W11, Tied for 1-Seed · Tatum 29/7/5 Lifts Celtics W6 · Luka 32/7/9 Leads Lakers W4 · Maxey 33/6 Sixers Bounce Back · Haliburton 25/10 Pacers Rout Bucks
item.description: Victor Wembanyama dominates with 28 points, 12 rebounds, 5 assists, and 4 blocks as San Antonio beats Minnesota 121-114 to extend their win streak to 11 and pull tied for the 1-seed; Jayson Tatum scores 29 with 7 boards and 5 assists as Boston tops Detroit 108-102 for their sixth straight; Luka Dončić erupts for 32/7/9 as the Lakers beat Houston 119-108 for win number four in a row; Tyrese Maxey pours in 33 with 6 assists as Philadelphia bounces back with a 116-112 win over Toronto; Tyrese Haliburton dishes 10 assists to go with 25 points as Indiana blows out Milwaukee 126-105 — Victor Wembanyama put on a two-way masterclass. The 22-year-old posted 28 points, 12 rebounds, 5 assists, and 4 blocks as the Spurs beat the Timberwolves 121-114 to extend their winning streak to 11 games and pull tied for the Western Conference 1-seed. Jayson Tatum led Boston past Detroit 108-102 with 29 points, 7 rebounds, and 5 assists, pushing the Celtics' win streak to 6 and opening a 2-game gap atop the East. Luka Dončić torched Houston for 32 points, 7 rebounds, and 9 assists as the Lakers cruised 119-108 for their fourth straight win. Tyrese Maxey bounced back with 33 points and 6 assists in Philadelphia's 116-112 victory over Toronto. Tyrese Haliburton had 25 points and 10 assists as Indiana routed Milwaukee 126-105.

item.title: Jokić 34/14/11 Triple-Double Sinks OKC Again · Brunson 34/8 Pushes Knicks to 50 Wins · Trae 31/11 Sparks Hawks Past Sixers · Booker 35 Ends Warriors' Season
item.description: Nikola Jokić posts his second triple-double in a week against OKC as Denver wins 124-119 to extend streak to 8; Jalen Brunson scores 34 with 8 assists as Knicks hit 50 wins; Trae Young's 31/11 lifts Atlanta past Philadelphia in play-in battle; Devin Booker erupts for 35 as Suns crush Warriors 122-101 — Nikola Jokić owned OKC for the second time in a week. The three-time MVP posted 34 points, 14 rebounds, and 11 assists as Denver beat the Thunder 124-119 at Ball Arena, extending their winning streak to 8 games. Jamal Murray added 22 and 6 assists. SGA had 31/5/8 in a losing effort as OKC (59-18) dropped their second straight. Denver (50-28) is now just half a game behind for the West's top seed. Jalen Brunson poured in 34 points with 8 assists as the Knicks beat Cleveland 115-109 to hit the 50-win mark. Trae Young's 31/11 performance lifted Atlanta past Philadelphia 121-117 in a critical play-in positioning game. Paolo Banchero's 26/8/6 led Orlando past Charlotte 104-98 in a must-win. Devin Booker torched Golden State for 35 points in a 122-101 blowout that effectively ended the Warriors' season.

item.title: Tatum 33/8/6 Torches Detroit in East Showdown · Wemby 30/11/3BLK Powers Spurs to W10 · Edwards Returns With 26/5/5 · Luka 38/11 in Lakers' Rout
item.description: Jayson Tatum scores 33 with 8 rebounds and 6 assists as Boston downs Detroit 112-104 in the East 1-vs-2 showdown; Wembanyama posts 30/11/4/3BLK as Spurs extend streak to 10; Anthony Edwards returns from injury with 26/5/5 as Wolves top Dallas; Luka erupts for 38/11 in Lakers' 128-116 blowout of Portland — The East's biggest game of the season belonged to Jayson Tatum. The Celtics star poured in 33 points on 12-of-21 shooting with 8 rebounds and 6 assists as Boston beat Detroit 112-104 on ESPN, cutting the Pistons' East lead to just 3 games. Jalen Duren had 24/13 for Detroit but the Pistons lacked shot creation without Cunningham. Victor Wembanyama was otherworldly with 30/11/4/3BLK as San Antonio beat Houston 119-112 for their 10th straight win. Anthony Edwards made his triumphant return, scoring 26/5/5 as Minnesota topped Dallas 117-109. Luka Dončić exploded for 38/11 as the Lakers steamrolled Portland 128-116 to hit 50 wins. Tyler Herro's 28 and 6 assists fueled Miami's 109-105 win over Toronto.

item.title: Denver Ends OKC's Streak! Jokić 31/13/9 Outduels SGA's 37 · Brunson 31 Bounces Back vs Orlando · Celtics Win 4th Straight · Clippers W6
item.description: Nikola Jokić scores 31 with 13 rebounds and 9 assists as Denver stuns Oklahoma City 118-115, snapping the Thunder's winning streak; Jalen Brunson erupts for 31 and 7 assists as the Knicks handle Orlando 114-101; Tatum's 27 leads Boston past Miami 118-105 for their 4th straight; Clippers extend streak to 6 behind Garland's 25 — The biggest result of the NBA season so far: Denver ended Oklahoma City's winning streak with a thrilling 118-115 victory at Ball Arena. Nikola Jokić was magnificent — 31 points, 13 rebounds, 9 assists on 12-of-19 shooting — outdueling Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's heroic 37-point effort. Jamal Murray's 26 points and clutch late three sealed it. Denver (49-28) has won seven straight. Jalen Brunson bounced back from two quiet games with 31 points and 7 assists as the Knicks rolled Orlando 114-101. Jayson Tatum had 27 as Boston beat Miami 118-105 for their fourth straight. Evan Mobley posted 25/12 as Cleveland topped Charlotte 119-108. Nickeil Alexander-Walker had 24 as Atlanta routed Washington 126-101. Alperen Sengun had 22/11/8 as Houston beat Chicago 121-109 for their third straight. The Clippers extended their streak to six behind Garland's 25 and Kawhi's 21.

item.title: SGA 34 Hammers Knicks on NBC · Jokić Triple-Double Powers Nuggets' 6th Straight · Tatum 31 Keeps Celtics Rolling · Clippers Win 5th Straight
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scores 34 with 8 assists as OKC dismantles New York 121-112 on NBC Peacock; Jokić records 28/14/11 triple-double in Denver's 131-115 rout of Golden State; Tatum drops 31 as Boston beats Charlotte 116-107; Garland's 28/9ast leads Clippers past Bucks for 5th straight win — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander put on an MVP showcase on national television, scoring 34 points on 12-of-22 shooting with 8 assists as the Thunder dismantled the Knicks 121-112 at Paycom Center. SGA scored 14 in the third quarter to blow the game open. Jalen Williams added 22 and Chet Holmgren had 16/9/4blk. Brunson fought with 29/8ast but the Knicks shot 31% from three. Nikola Jokić recorded his third triple-double in four games — 28/14/11 on 11-17 shooting — as Denver rolled Golden State 131-115 for their sixth straight win. Murray added 24, MPJ had 19. Porziņģis led GSW with 26 but the Warriors fell to 36-39. Jayson Tatum scored 31 (12-21, 4-8 3PT) as Boston beat Charlotte 116-107 for their third straight. Knueppel bounced back with 24 (5-10 3PT). Darius Garland had 28/9ast and Kawhi added 24/7/5 as the Clippers crushed Milwaukee 118-99 for their fifth straight. Scottie Barnes posted 24/6/9 as Toronto edged Orlando 112-106 in a pivotal play-in matchup. Bam Adebayo had 24/12 as Miami beat Indiana 119-108. Jabari Smith Jr. had 23/11 as Houston topped New Orleans 120-109.

item.title: Castle Triple-Double Powers Spurs' 8th Straight · Embiid Rallies 76ers Past Hornets · Giddey's Triple-Double Wasted in 1-Point Loss
item.description: Stephon Castle posts 22/10/10 on 9-of-13 shooting as San Antonio demolishes Milwaukee 127-95 for their 8th consecutive win; Embiid scores 29 and George adds 26/13 as Philadelphia rallies from 13 down to snap Charlotte's 5-game streak; Giddey's 18/13/10 triple-double goes to waste in Chicago's 125-124 heartbreaker at Memphis — Stephon Castle announced himself with a 22-point, 10-rebound, 10-assist triple-double on 9-of-13 shooting as the Spurs obliterated Milwaukee 127-95 for their eighth straight win. Victor Wembanyama added 23/15/6 and San Antonio (56-18) pulled within 2 games of OKC. Detroit dominated Minnesota 109-87 behind Harris's 18 and Duren's 10/13/5 — the Pistons are 5-1 without Cunningham (collapsed lung). Joel Embiid scored 29, Paul George had 26/13, and Tyrese Maxey added 26/8ast as Philadelphia rallied from 13 down to beat Charlotte 118-114, snapping the Hornets' 5-game win streak. Josh Giddey posted a gorgeous 18/13/10 triple-double but Chicago fell to Memphis 125-124 — Cedric Coward's 24/9/4 lifted the Grizzlies. Jalen Green erupted for 31 (13-22, 5-11 3PT) as Phoenix crushed Utah 134-109. The Hawks rolled past Sacramento 123-113 behind NAW's 27/8ast and Jalen Johnson's 26/10ast.

item.title: Cleveland 149, Miami 128 — Strus 8 Threes vs Former Team · Jokić 33/15/12 on 13-of-16 · Kawhi Game-Winner · Luka 41
item.description: Max Strus torches former team with 8-of-11 from three as Cleveland demolishes Miami and breaks the three-way play-in tie; Jokić records another triple-double on absurd 81.3% shooting; Kawhi Leonard hits game-winner to cap 21-point comeback; Dončić drops 41 as Lakers cruise past Nets; Warriors clinch Play-In berth — Cleveland turned Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse into a crime scene. The Cavaliers scored 149 points — their highest-scoring game of the season — to annihilate the Heat 149-128 in a result that reshapes the Eastern Conference play-in race. Max Strus torched his former team with 29 points on 8-of-11 from three, Evan Mobley had 23/10, Jarrett Allen added 18/10, and James Harden dished a season-high 14 assists. Miami (39-35) dropped out of the three-way play-in tie. Nikola Jokić shot 13-of-16 for 33/15/12 as Denver beat Utah 135-129 for their fifth straight — two days after his near-historic 23/21/19. Jamal Murray had 31/14. Kawhi Leonard hit the game-winning shot to cap a stunning comeback from 21 points down in Q1 as the Clippers edged Indiana 114-113 — Darius Garland had 30. Luka Dončić scored 41 (15-25 FG) as the Lakers handled Brooklyn 116-99. Payton Pritchard erupted for 36 off the bench to lead Boston past Atlanta 109-102. SGA scored 25 despite going 0-for-10 from three as OKC beat Chicago 131-113. The Warriors clinched a Play-In berth by beating Washington 131-126 behind Porziņģis's 28. Scottie Barnes had 23/6/12 as Toronto topped New Orleans 119-106. Kevin Durant posted 25/10ast as Houston beat Memphis 119-109. Cooper Flagg scored 24 as Dallas beat Portland 100-93.

item.title: Knueppel Makes History — Youngest to 250 Threes · Banchero 30 Snaps Magic Skid · Duren 30/10/7 Without Cunningham
item.description: Kon Knueppel scores 26 with 6 three-pointers to become the youngest player in NBA history with 250 threes in a season; Banchero's 30/9/7 ends Orlando's 6-game losing streak; Duren dominates as Pistons cruise without Cunningham — Kon Knueppel wrote himself into the NBA record books. The 20-year-old scored 26 points with six three-pointers — becoming the youngest player in NBA history to hit 250 threes in a single season — while nearly posting a triple-double (11 rebounds, 8 assists) as the Hornets dismantled the Knicks 114-103, snapping New York's 7-game winning streak. Charlotte (39-34) has won five straight. Jalen Duren posted 30/10/7 on 10-of-12 shooting as the Pistons beat the Pelicans 129-108 — their third win in four games without Cade Cunningham (collapsed lung). Paolo Banchero had 30/9/7 as the Magic ended a six-game skid with a 121-117 win over Sacramento. Three teams — Orlando, Charlotte, and Miami — are now tied at 39-34 in the East play-in race.

item.title: Celtics End OKC's 12-Game Streak · Embiid & George Return With 63 Combined · Jokic's Historic 23/21/19 · Luka 43 · Hawks OT Upset in Detroit
item.description: Jaylen Brown scores 31 as Boston ends Oklahoma City's 12-game winning streak 119-109; Joel Embiid (35) and Paul George (28) return together as Sixers score 157; Jokić nearly records first-ever 20-20-20 game with 23/21/19; Luka drops 43 on Indiana; Hawks stun Pistons in OT — The night the streaks died. Jaylen Brown scored 31 points — 14 in a pivotal third quarter — as the Celtics rallied from 13 down to beat Oklahoma City 119-109, snapping the Thunder's 12-game winning streak. Jayson Tatum added 19 and 12 rebounds. Joel Embiid returned from injury with 35 points and Paul George had 28 in his first game back from a 25-game suspension as the Sixers scored 157 against Chicago — their highest-scoring game of the season. Nikola Jokic had one of the most absurd stat lines in NBA history — 23 points, 21 rebounds, 19 assists — falling one assist short of the first 20-20-20 game in NBA history as Denver beat Dallas 142-135. Luka Doncic dropped 43 on 15-of-30 shooting as the Lakers beat Indiana 137-130. CJ McCollum scored 27 with the tiebreaking 3-point play in OT as the Hawks stunned the Pistons 130-129 — Atlanta's 14th win in 15 games. Minnesota edged Houston 110-108 in overtime. Miami topped Cleveland 120-103.

item.title: SGA Extends Streak to 131 · Jokić 23/17/17 Edges Suns · Knicks Snap Suns' Home Streak · Thunder W12 · Spurs Cruise
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander pushes his consecutive 20-point game record to 131 as OKC wins their 12th straight; Jokic posts 23/17/17 in Denver's 125-123 win at Phoenix; Brunson's 27 leads Knicks past Suns; Spurs extend their win streak to six — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander extended his all-time record to 131 consecutive 20-point games as the Thunder won their 12th straight. The MVP frontrunner continues to set records that may never be broken. Nikola Jokic had another absurd triple-double — 23 points, 17 rebounds, 17 assists — as Denver edged Phoenix 125-123 on the road. The Suns dropped to 40-32 and continue to slide. Jalen Brunson scored 27 as the Knicks beat the Suns 119-112 — a result that further complicates Phoenix's postseason positioning. The Spurs cruised to their sixth straight win behind Wembanyama's all-around excellence. Luka Doncic had 32 points in a 113-110 loss at Detroit on the previous night, as the Lakers' road trip hit a bump.

item.title: Luka's 38 Powers Lakers Past Rockets · Hawks Win 11th Straight · SGA's 130th Consecutive 20+ Game · Tatum 31 Routs Warriors
item.description: Luka Dončić scores 38 with a clutch step-back three as the Lakers beat Houston 118-115 for their seventh straight win; Hawks beat Dallas for their 11th consecutive victory; SGA cruises to 25 in three quarters as OKC demolishes Brooklyn; Tatum drops 31 as Celtics handle Curry-less Warriors — Luka Dončić scored 38 points on 13-of-24 shooting — including a step-back three with 47 seconds left — as the Lakers beat the Rockets 118-115 in Houston for their seventh consecutive win. Anthony Davis added 24 and 13 rebounds. SGA extended his record to 130 consecutive 20-point games with 25 in three quarters as OKC rolled Brooklyn 121-98 for their 11th straight. The Hawks won their 11th straight behind Jalen Johnson's 28/9/7 in a 122-116 win in Dallas — Cooper Flagg had 25/8 in defeat. Tatum scored 31 as Boston crushed Golden State 118-101 — the Warriors dropped below .500. Portland beat Indiana 116-105 behind Avdija's 22. Toronto topped Chicago 119-111 as Ingram scored 28 for the third straight game. Minnesota cruised past Utah 120-104 without Edwards. The Clippers survived in New Orleans 112-106 with Kawhi on a minutes restriction. Denver beat Memphis 119-105 as Jokic had 22/11/9.

item.title: Luka's 38 Powers Lakers Past Rockets · Hawks Win 11th Straight · SGA's 130th Consecutive 20+ Game · Tatum 31 Routs Warriors
item.description: Luka Dončić scores 38 with a clutch step-back three as the Lakers beat Houston 118-115 for their seventh straight win; Hawks beat Dallas for their 11th consecutive victory; SGA cruises to 25 in three quarters as OKC demolishes Brooklyn; Tatum drops 31 as Celtics handle Curry-less Warriors — Luka Dončić scored 38 points on 13-of-24 shooting — including a step-back three with 47 seconds left — as the Lakers beat the Rockets 118-115 in Houston for their seventh consecutive win. Anthony Davis added 24 and 13 rebounds. SGA extended his record to 130 consecutive 20-point games with 25 in three quarters as OKC rolled Brooklyn 121-98 for their 11th straight. The Hawks won their 11th straight behind Jalen Johnson's 28/9/7 in a 122-116 win in Dallas — Cooper Flagg had 25/8 in defeat. Tatum scored 31 as Boston crushed Golden State 118-101 — the Warriors dropped below .500. Portland beat Indiana 116-105 behind Avdija's 22. Toronto topped Chicago 119-111 as Ingram scored 28 for the third straight game. Minnesota cruised past Utah 120-104 without Edwards. The Clippers survived in New Orleans 112-106 with Kawhi on a minutes restriction. Denver beat Memphis 119-105 as Jokic had 22/11/9.

item.title: Wembanyama 33/11/5 BLK Dismantles Suns · Cunningham Returns for 24 · Banchero Outduels Mobley · Luka 36 Leads Lakers Rout
item.description: Victor Wembanyama posts 33 points, 11 rebounds, and 5 blocks as the Spurs rout Phoenix 129-108; Cade Cunningham returns from back spasms with 24 points and 8 assists; Banchero edges Mobley in Orlando; Luka drops 36 in a home blowout of Sacramento — Victor Wembanyama was a force of nature in San Antonio — 33 points, 11 rebounds, and 5 blocks as the Spurs routed Phoenix 129-108. De'Aaron Fox added 28 points and the Spurs (52-18) remain the West's second-best team. Kevin Durant scored 26 in the loss but the Suns fell to .500 in their last 10. The injury story of the night was a good one: Cade Cunningham made a triumphant return from his back spasms, posting 24 points, 8 assists, and 5 rebounds as Detroit beat New Orleans 121-103. Duren added 18 and 10. Cade looked healthy and explosive. Paolo Banchero had 27 points and 9 rebounds as Orlando topped Cleveland 118-113 — Evan Mobley countered with 25 and 12. Luka Doncic dropped 36 with 9 rebounds and 8 assists as the Lakers blew out Sacramento 131-109 at Crypto.com Arena — Austin Reaves added 24. Brooklyn upset Chicago 109-101 behind Nic Claxton's 22 and 12 to snap a 4-game skid.

item.title: SGA Extends Streak to 130 · Thunder 11 Straight · Luka 35 Edges Houston Thriller · Hawks Make It 11 · Jokic Triple-Double
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 27 before resting in the fourth as OKC rolls Brooklyn for their 11th consecutive win; Luka ices a road thriller in Houston with a pull-up three with 18 seconds left; Atlanta extends their run to 11 straight games — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander extended his all-time record to 130 consecutive 20-point games — and barely broke a sweat doing it. SGA scored 27 in three quarters, then sat the fourth as OKC routed Brooklyn 119-97 for their 11th consecutive win. The Thunder (55-15) are the undisputed class of the NBA. Luka Doncic was brilliant in Houston — 35 points, 8 rebounds, and 10 assists — as the Lakers held off the Rockets 120-117 on the road, iced by Luka's pull-up three with 18 seconds left. Los Angeles (44-25) has now won seven straight. The Celtics handled the Curry-less Warriors 121-104 in Boston — Jayson Tatum had 24 and Jaylen Brown added 21. Nikola Jokic posted his 28th triple-double of the season (19 points, 14 rebounds, 11 assists) as Denver beat Memphis 116-104. The Hawks made it 11 straight — Nickeil Alexander-Walker had 25 as Atlanta escaped Dallas 121-115 despite Cooper Flagg's 26 points, 9 rebounds, and 6 assists. Minnesota beat Utah 122-101 without Edwards — Randle had 28 and Hyland 24 off the bench. Brandon Ingram dropped 30 as Toronto beat Chicago 115-108. The Clippers held the 8-seed with a 110-103 win in New Orleans — Kawhi played 24 limited minutes and scored 22. Portland beat Indiana 114-103 behind Deni Avdija's 24 points.

item.title: SGA's 40 Pushes Streak to 129 · Cunningham Back Spasms Shake East · Hart 12-for-13 Without Brunson · LaMelo 30/13 · Edwards Out Weeks
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander drops 40 as Thunder win tenth straight; Cunningham exits after 5 minutes with back spasms; Edwards diagnosed with knee inflammation — out 1-2 weeks; Hart goes 12-for-13 as Knicks blow out Indiana without Brunson; LaMelo Ball's 30/13 demolishes Heat in Charlotte's biggest win of the season — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander dropped 40 points on 14-of-27 shooting to extend his all-time record to 129 consecutive 20-point games as the Thunder beat the Magic 113-108 for their tenth straight win. Cade Cunningham exited after just five minutes with back spasms in Detroit's 130-117 win over Washington — Jalen Duren had a career-high 36 points. Anthony Edwards was diagnosed with right knee inflammation and will miss 1-2 weeks. Giannis Antetokounmpo was officially diagnosed with a bone bruise and won't be re-evaluated until March 28. Josh Hart went 12-for-13 (5-5 3PT) for 33 points as the Knicks crushed Indiana 136-110 without Brunson. LaMelo Ball had 30 and 13 assists as Charlotte blew out Miami 136-106. Evan Mobley posted 27/15 as Cleveland beat the Bucks 123-116. The Spurs rolled Sacramento 132-104 behind Wemby's 18/8.

item.title: Hawks Make It 10 Straight — Magic Streak Ends at 7 · Giannis MRI: Bone Bruise Confirmed, Out Until March 28 · Curry Diagnosed With Runner's Knee · Cunningham 27 Leads Pistons
item.description: Atlanta's Alexander-Walker drops 28 to end Orlando's 7-game run and push the Hawks to 10 consecutive wins; Antetokounmpo officially diagnosed with bone bruise on hyperextended left knee; Golden State confirms Curry out indefinitely with bilateral runner's knee — The Atlanta Hawks made it 10 consecutive wins — beating the Orlando Magic 108-102 to end Orlando's own 7-game win streak in the process. Nickeil Alexander-Walker dropped 28 points with 5-of-9 from three and Jalen Johnson had 21 points and 11 rebounds. A light Monday slate of four games was dominated by injury news. Giannis Antetokounmpo's MRI results were officially released: bone bruise on the hyperextended left knee — he will not be re-evaluated until March 28 and will miss at least his next five games. In Milwaukee's 116-104 win over Philadelphia, Kevin Porter Jr. stepped up with 26 points and 10 assists as Milwaukee's primary creator. Golden State separately announced Stephen Curry has been diagnosed with bilateral runner's knee with no return date given — casting serious doubt over the Warriors' play-in chances. Cade Cunningham bounced back emphatically from his early exit against Washington — 27 points and 8 assists as Detroit beat Houston 118-109. Jalen Duren added 21 points and 10 rebounds. The Pistons (49-18) maintained the East's best record. Brandon Ingram scored 31 as Toronto beat Memphis 119-107 — the Grizzlies fell to their seventh consecutive loss, and Ja Morant remains without a return timetable.

item.title: SGA Barely Survives — 128 Straight · Giannis Hyperextends Knee · DeRozan 41/11 · Flagg's Revenge · Brunson's 21-Point Comeback
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander scores exactly 20 to extend record to 128 games; Antetokounmpo hyperextends knee in Bucks win; Brunson leads Knicks back from 21 down on ABC; Flagg drops 27/10 in Cleveland revenge game — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored exactly 20 points with 10 assists — his closest call — to extend his record to 128 consecutive 20-point games as OKC beat Minnesota 116-103. Giannis Antetokounmpo scored 31/14/8 before hyperextending his left knee on a dunk — MRI pending — as the Bucks beat Indiana 134-123. Brunson scored 30 with 9 assists (10-10 FT) to lead the Knicks back from 21 down to beat the Warriors 110-107 on ABC. Cooper Flagg had 27/10/6 as Dallas got revenge on Cleveland 130-120, two days after a 33-point loss in the same building. DeMar DeRozan posted a season-high 41/11 as the Kings beat Utah 116-111. Brandon Ingram scored 34 as Toronto beat East-leading Detroit 119-108. Quentin Grimes had 31 as the shorthanded Sixers beat Portland 109-103.

item.title: Luka's OT Dagger Beats Nuggets · Reaves' Free-Throw Trick · Hawks Win 9th Straight · Wemby Returns With 32/12/8
item.description: Doncic hits game-winning jumper with 0.5 seconds left in OT as Lakers beat Nuggets 127-125; Reaves forces overtime by rebounding his intentionally missed FT; McCollum passes Pierce; Banchero beats Heat for 5th time — Luka Doncic hit a fall-away jumper with 0.5 seconds left in overtime as the Lakers beat the Nuggets 127-125 on ABC. Austin Reaves forced OT by intentionally missing a free throw, grabbing the rebound, and hitting a floater with 1.9 seconds left. Luka had 30/11/13 and Reaves added 32. Jokic had 24/16/14 but Murray went 1-14 and fouled out 31 seconds into OT. CJ McCollum scored 30 with 7-10 from three — surpassing Paul Pierce for 16th all-time in 3PM — as the Hawks beat the Bucks 122-99 for their 9th straight win. Wembanyama returned with 32/12/8/3BLK as the Spurs beat the Hornets 115-102. Banchero had 27/8/7 as the Magic beat the Heat 121-117 for their 7th straight (5-0 vs MIA). Tatum had 20/14 as the Celtics beat the Wizards 111-100. The Kings shocked the Clippers 118-109 behind Westbrook's 209th triple-double.

item.title: Edwards Erupts for 42 · Mobley's 29 Destroys Dallas · Duren 30/13 · Ingram's 36-Point Comeback · Brunson 29
item.description: Anthony Edwards scores 42 as Timberwolves hold off Warriors 127-117; Evan Mobley matches season-high 29 (12-15 FG) as Cavaliers crush Mavericks 138-105; Pistons hand Grizzlies 7th straight loss — Anthony Edwards scored 42 points (13-22 FG, 12-12 FT) with 8 rebounds and 5 assists as the Timberwolves beat the Warriors 127-117. Evan Mobley had 29 points on 12-15 shooting as the Cavaliers demolished Dallas 138-105 — Cooper Flagg scored 25 in the loss. Jalen Brunson scored 29 and Mitchell Robinson had a career-high 22 rebounds as the Knicks beat Indiana 101-92 — the Pacers' 12th straight loss. Jalen Duren had 30 points and 13 rebounds as the Pistons beat Memphis 126-110 for the Grizzlies' 7th straight loss. Brandon Ingram scored 36 as the Raptors rallied to beat the Suns 122-115. Kevin Durant's 18-footer with 7.6 seconds left lifted Houston over New Orleans 107-105. Kawhi scored 28 as the Clippers beat the Bulls 119-108. Portland beat Utah 124-114 behind Henderson, Holiday, and Clingan.

item.title: SGA Breaks Wilt's Record — 127 Games · Luka Career-High 51 · Jokic 31/20/12 Monster Triple-Double
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 35 to break Chamberlain's 63-year-old record of consecutive 20-point games; Doncic erupts for 51 as Lakers torch Bulls 142-130; Jokic rallies Denver from 20 down — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 35 points to break Wilt Chamberlain's 63-year-old record with his 127th consecutive 20-point game as OKC edged Boston 104-102. Luka Doncic posted a career-high 51 points as the Lakers torched the Bulls 142-130. Nikola Jokic had 31/20/12 and Jamal Murray added 39 as Denver rallied from 20 down to beat San Antonio 136-131. Bam Adebayo scored 21 and Larsson added 28 as Miami won its 7th straight over Milwaukee 112-105. Devin Booker scored 43 and Jeff Green had 36 in Phoenix's 123-108 win over Indiana. Jalen Suggs scored 28 as Orlando won its 6th straight in OT over Washington 136-131. Khris Middleton scored 35 (22 in Q4) as Dallas snapped an 8-game losing streak over Memphis 120-112.
